actions-list.actionItem: The House adjourned. The next meeting is scheduled for 10:00 a.m. on March 26, 2026.
On motion to adjourn Agreed to by voice vote.
Mr. Carter (GA) moved that the House do now adjourn.
The House has concluded all anticipated legislative business and has proceeded to Special Order speeches.
The House proceeded with further one minute speeches.
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.

actions-list.header: On passage Passed by the Yeas and Nays: 218
On motion to recommit Failed by the Yeas and Nays: 207

actions-list.actionItem: The previous question on the motion to recommit was ordered pursuant to clause 2(b) of rule XIX.
Ms. McClellan moved to recommit to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
The previous question was ordered pursuant to the rule.
The House proceeded with one hour of debate on H.R. 5103.

Rule provides for consideration of H.R. 8029, H. Res. 1128, H.R. 5103 and H.R. 7084. The resolution provides for consideration of H.R. 8029, H. Res. 1128, H.R. 5103, and H.R. 7084 under a closed rule with one hour of general debate on each measure. The resolution provides for one motion to recommit on H.R. 8029, H.R. 5103, and H.R. 7084.
Considered under the provisions of rule H. Res. 1131. H.R. 5103 — "To establish a program to Beautify the District of Columbia and establish the District of Columbia Safe and Beautiful Commission."

actions-list.header: The Speaker laid before the House a message from the President transmitting a notification stating that the national emergency declared in Executive Order 13694, with respect to significant malicious cyber-enabled activities, of April 1, 2015, and subsequent executive orders, is to continue in effect beyond April 1, 2026
actions-list.actionItem: referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs and ordered to be printed (H. Doc. 119-145).

actions-list.header: The Speaker laid before the House a message from the President transmitting a notification stating that the national emergency declared in Executive Order 13664 of April 3, 2014, with respect to South Sudan is to continue in effect beyond April 3, 2026
actions-list.actionItem: referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs and ordered to be printed (H. Doc. 119-144).

actions-list.header: The Speaker laid before the House a message from the President transmitting a notification stating that the national emergency with respect to specified harmful foreign activities of the Government of the Russian Federation declared in Executive Order 14024 of April 15, 2021, which was expanded in scope in subsequent executive orders, is to continue in effect beyond April 15, 2026
actions-list.actionItem: referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs and ordered to be printed (H. Doc. 119-143).

actions-list.header: The Speaker laid before the House a message from the President transmitting a notification stating that the national emergency declared in Executive Order 14257 of April 2, 2025, is to continue in effect beyond April 2, 2026
actions-list.actionItem: referred to the Committees on Foreign Affairs and Ways and Means and ordered to be printed (H. Doc. 119-142).
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.

actions-list.header: On agreeing to the resolution Agreed to by recorded vote: 214
actions-list.actionItem: 210 (Roll no. 99).

actions-list.header: On ordering the previous question Agreed to by the Yeas and Nays: 212
actions-list.actionItem: 210 (Roll no. 98).

Considered as unfinished business. H. Res. 1131 — "Providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 8029) making appropriations for the Department of Homeland Security for the fiscal year ending September 30, 2026, and for other purposes; providing for consideration of the resolution (H. Res. 1128) expressing the support of the House of Representatives for the Department of Homeland Security; providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 5103) to establish a program to Beautify the District of Columbia and establish the District of Columbia Safe and Beautiful Commission; providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 7084) to amend title 46, United States Code, with respect to the types of vessels that may enter or operate in navigable waters of the United States or transfer cargo in any port or place under the jurisdiction of the United States, and for other purposes; and for other purposes."
The Chair announced the unfinished business was the question on ordering the previous on H. Res. 1131, and agreeing to the resolution, if ordered.
The House convened, returning from a recess continuing the legislative day of March 25.
The Speaker announced that the House do now recess. The next meeting is subject to the call of the Chair.

At the conclusion of debate on H. Res. 1131, the Chair put the question on ordering the previous question and by voice vote, announced that the ayes had prevailed. Mr. McGovern demanded the yeas and nays and the Chair postponed further proceedings until a time to be announced.
The House proceeded with one hour of debate on H. Res. 1131.

Considered as privileged matter. H. Res. 1131 — "Providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 8029) making appropriations for the Department of Homeland Security for the fiscal year ending September 30, 2026, and for other purposes; providing for consideration of the resolution (H. Res. 1128) expressing the support of the House of Representatives for the Department of Homeland Security; providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 5103) to establish a program to Beautify the District of Columbia and establish the District of Columbia Safe and Beautiful Commission; providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 7084) to amend title 46, United States Code, with respect to the types of vessels that may enter or operate in navigable waters of the United States or transfer cargo in any port or place under the jurisdiction of the United States, and for other purposes; and for other purposes."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On agreeing to the resolution Agreed to without objection.
Considered as privileged matter. H. Res. 1135 — "Electing Members to certain standing committees of the House of Representatives."
The House received a communication from the Honorable Richard McCormick. Mr. McCormick submitted his resignation from the Committee on Foreign Affairs. The resignation was accepted without objection.
The House received a communication from the Honorable Nick LaLota. Mr. LaLota submitted his resignation from the Committee on Small Business. The resignation was accepted without objection.

The House received a communication from the Honorable Max L. Miller. Mr. Miller (OH) submitted his resignation from the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology. The resignation was accepted without objection.
The House received a communication from the Honorable Troy Downing. Mr. Downing submitted his resignation from the Committee on Small Business. The resignation was accepted without objection.

actions-list.header: Commission to Study the Potential Creation of a National Museum of Asian Pacific American History and Culture
actions-list.actionItem: Pursuant to section 2(b)(2) of the Commission to Study the Potential Creation of a National Museum of Asian Pacific American History and Culture Act (Public Law 117-140), and the order of the House of January 3, 2025, the Speaker appointed Mr. Anh "Joseph" Cao, Metairie, Louisiana, to fill the existing vacancy.
The House proceeded with one minute speeches which by direction of the Chair, would be limited to 15 per side of the aisle.
The Chair designated Mr. Burchett to lead the Members in reciting the Pledge of Allegiance to the Flag.
The Speaker announced approval of the Journal.  Pursuant to clause 1, rule I, the Journal stands approved.
Today's prayer was offered by the House Chaplain, Chaplain Margaret Grun Kibben.
The House convened, returning from a recess continuing the legislative day of March 25.
The Speaker announced that the House do now recess. The next meeting is scheduled for 12:00 P.M. today.
The House proceeded with Morning-Hour Debate. At the conclusion of Morning-Hour, the House will recess until 12:00 p.m. for the start of legislative business.
The Speaker designated the Honorable Riley M. Moore to act as Speaker pro tempore for today.
The House convened, starting a new legislative day.
